(CORRECTED) REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE BY PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS TO DEMURRER BY RESPONDENTS; POS FILED BY PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 MOTION ON WRIT OF MANDATE, INJUNCTIVE AND DECLARATORY RELIEF BY SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000, ET AL. CONTINUED FROM SEP-01-2009 TO LAW AND MOTION AT SEP-10-2009, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED. THIS COURT HAS THE POWER TO RULE ON THE INSTANT PETITIONS, AS JUDGE BUSCH'S ORDER WAS LIMITED TO ADJUDICATING THE RIGHTS OF THE CASE UNION. THIS COURT HAS INDEPENDENTLY REVIEWED THE RECORD AND AUTHORITIES IN THIS CASE AND FINDS THE DOCTRINE OF EXCLUSIVE CONCURRENT JURISDICTION DOES NOT APPLY. THE SPECIFIC EXEMPTION UNDER INSURANCE CODE SECTION 11873(C) FOR NON-EXECUTIVE EMPLOYEES WAS NOT ADJUDICATED IN THE SACRAMENTO CASE. INSURANCE CODE SECTION 11873(C) EXEMPTS STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND EMPLOYEES FROM THE FURLOUGH. A "FURLOUGH" REDUCES THE AVAILABILITY OF STAFF AND CONSTITUTES A "STAFF CUTBACK." THE PETITION IS GRANTED AND THE WRIT SHALL ISSUE. FURTHER: THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 10, 2009, FOR (1) FINAL ORDER ON TODAY'S HEARING, AND (2) ISSUE OF LIFTING THE AUTOMATIC STAY. ANY ADDITIONAL BRIEFING REGARDING THESE ISSUES TO BE FILED BY SEPTEMBER 8, 2009, WITH COURTESY COPIES TO THE COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Re-Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Leave To Intervene In Jan Frank'S Verified X-Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Writ Of Prohibition And X-Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Oct-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: INTERVENOR STATIONARY ENGINEERS LOCAL 39 INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS, AFL-CIO'S RE-NOTICE OF HEARING ON MOTION FOR LEAVE TO INTERVENE IN JAN FRANK'S VERIFIED CROSS-PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE OR WRIT OF PROHIBITION AND CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - DENY, MOTION IS UNTIMELY. SEE CCP 387; ALLEN V. CALIFORNIA WATER & TELEPHONE CO., (1947)31 CAL.2D 104, 108. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Intervene In Jan Frank'S Verified Cross-Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Writ Of Prohibition And Cross Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief
Off Calendar Oct-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: STATIONARY ENGINEERS LOCAL 39 INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS, AFL-CIO'S Motion For Leave To Intervene In Jan Frank's Verified Cross-Petition For Writ Of Mandate Or Writ Of Prohibition And Cross-Complaint For Declaratory And Injunctive Relief.
RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Continued to October 27, 2009, per amended notice of motion. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Motion Re Form Of Judgment (On Court'S Own Motion)
Off Calendar Sep-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Motion regarding form of judgment (on the court's own motion.) Appearances: J. Felix De La Torre, Esq. and Monica Ahuja, Esq., counsel for SEIU Local 1000 (916) 554-1279, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Ronald B. Turovsky, Esq. Manatt, Phelps & Phillips LLP (310) 312-4249, appearing for Cross-Petitioner Jan Frank; The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: The court adopts SEIU's judgment. SEIU to prepare a writ. The Petition prayed for back pay with legal interest. Back pay is appropriate to make petitioners whole. Interest owed at legal rate since damages are certain by calculation. Judgment signed in open Court. Peremptory Writ of Mandate also signed, and that document will be filed in the Clerk's Office. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Upon Appeal By Service Employees International Union, Local 1000,Request For Judicial Noticem Lodgment Of Foreign Authorities In Support Of Petitioner Service Employees Internatioal Union, Local 1000'S Motion To Lift Automatic Stay Upon Appeal
Off Calendar Sep-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 Motion to Lift Stay upon Appeal. Appearances: Paul Harris, Chief Counsel, SEIU Local 1000 (916) 554-1279, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Ronald B. Turovsky, Esq. Manatt, Phelps & Phillips LLP (310) 312-4249, appearing for Cross-Petitioner Jan Frank; Will M. Yamada, (916) 324-0512 Dept. of Personnel Adm., State of California; and, David W. Tyra, Esq. of Kronick Moskovitz Tiedemann & Girard, (916) 321-4500, appearing for Respondents. (This tentative ruling on this matter is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to lift stay granted. Plaintiffs demonstrate irreparable harm if stay is not lifted. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion On Writ Of Mandate, Injunctive And Declaratory Relief By Service Employees International Union, Local 1000 Et Al, Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Sep-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Hearing on Proposed Final Order regarding PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 Motion on Writ of Mandate, Injunctive and Declaratory Relief by Service Employees International Union, Local 1000 et al. Appearances: Paul Harris, Chief Counsel, SEIU Local 1000 (916) 554-1279, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Ronald B. Turovsky, Esq. Manatt, Phelps & Phillips LLP (310) 312-4249, appearing for Cross-Petitioner Jan Frank; Will M. Yamada, (916) 324-0512 Dept. of Personnel Adm., State of California and opposing party; and, David W. Notice Of Motion And Motion On Writ Of Mandate, Injunctive And Declaratory Relief By Service Employees International Union, Local 1000 Et Al, Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Sep-01-2009 Continued To Sep-10-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 Motion on Writ of Mandate, Injunctive and Declaratory Relief by Service Employees International Union, Local 1000, et al. Appearances: J. Felix De La Torre, Esq. and Monica Ahuja, Esq. Staff Attorneys, Local 1000 SEIU, (916) 554-1279 appearing for SEI and moving party; Ronald B. Turovsky, Esq. of Manatt, Phelps & Phillips, LLC (310) 312-4249 appearing for Cross-Petitioner Jan Frank. Will M. Yamada, Esq. Counsel, State of California, Dept. of Personnel Adm., (916) 324-0512, appearing for Respondent and opposing party; David W. Tyra, Esq. of Kronick Moskovitz Tiedemann & Girard, appearing for Respondent and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted. This court has the power to rule on the instant petitions, as Judge Busch's order was limited to adjudicating the rights of the CASE Union. This court has independently reviewed the record and authorities in this case and finds the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction does not apply. The specific exemption under Insurance Code section 11873(c) for non-executive employees was not adjudicated in the Sacramento case. Insurance Code section 11873(c) exempts State Compensation Insurance Fund employees from the furlough. A "furlough" reduces the availability of staff and constitutes a "staff cutback." The petition is granted and the writ shall issue. Further: The matter is continued to September 10, 2009, for (1) Final Order on today's hearing, and (2) Issue of lifting the automatic stay. Any Additional briefing regarding these issues to be filed by September 8, 2009, with courtesy copies to the Court. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
